name: Starlight (Astro Documentation Framework)

performance.notes: Zero-JS by default (Astro islands); sub-100ms page loads

agent_readiness.notes: Astro-based documentation framework. Zero-JS by default (Astro islands for interactive components). Markdown/MDX content. File-based routing. Auto sidebar from file structure. i18n built in (multi-locale out of box). Pagefind search (client-side, works offline). Dark mode, responsive. Component overrides for customization. Expressive Code for syntax highlighting. Astro plugin ecosystem. The Astro team's official docs framework — used for Astro docs itself.

gotchas: Zero-JS means more work to add interactivity
best_when: Astro-based projects wanting zero-JS documentation sites
Multilingual documentation out of the box
Teams wanting excellent performance scores by default
Markdown-heavy docs without heavy JS frameworks
avoid_when: Non-Astro teams (Docusaurus or Fumadocs easier onboarding)
Need heavy React component integration in docs
summary: Starlight is Astro's official documentation framework. Zero-JS by default (Astro islands), file-based routing, auto sidebar, built-in i18n, Pagefind search, dark mode. Excellent performance. Markdown/MDX content. Used for Astro's own docs. Best for teams already in the Astro ecosystem or wanting maximum static performance. Docusaurus for React ecosystem; VitePress for Vue ecosystem.
